mentions.note: Recommended over Galaxy Digital for quantum exposure; cleaner play than crypto-linked companies.
Bessembinder research identified IBM as one of 17 mega-return stocks; long history but exposed to cyclicality and tech disruption.

Strong Q1 beat, reaffirmed full-year outlook. CEO Arvin Christian executing well on software-first and quantum computing initiatives. Core business solid despite macro uncertainty. Unfairly caught in sector negativity bias.
Blasted unfairly last quarter; strong fundamentals; expect reverse move this quarter with quantum computing progress updates.
Core quantum computing provider; terrific buyer for quantum exposure without standalone risk.
Terrific buyer; quantum computing viable player alongside Google and Honeywell.
Last quarter suspect but buy on this small pullback is right call; tech fundamentals support recovery.

Enterprise software/hardware company lost 13% after Anthropic press release suggesting AI could replicate IBM services. Complicated business hard for investors to understand. Vulnerable to AI displacement narrative despite solid fundamentals.
Fell 13% (worst day since 2000) on Anthropic's product announcement suggesting competition; actual threat level uncertain but market pricing in disruption to enterprise services.

Sales +12% (9.7B beat), EPS beat 4.31. Software (automation +18%, data +22%) and mainframe (Z17 +67%) driving double-digit growth. Consulting backlog 12.5B. 25x forward earnings reasonable premium. Room to run.
Magnificent earnings; software up 14%; very strong full-year forecast; inexpensive stock; CEO Arvin Krishna running company well; flying after hours.
Last quarter traders left street wrangled but stock recovered quickly. Expect same pattern this quarter; amazing company.

Tripled since late 2022, up 40% past 12 months; CEO Arvind Krishna top-notch; strong hybrid cloud, AI, consulting, quantum; strongest sales growth in years; broken above 50-day MA; MACD bullish crossover; OBV making new highs; RSI far from overbought.
